summary: Emit a loud stderr warning when Surf's underlying *http.Transport is nil instead of silently falling back to http.DefaultTransport.
reason: If a future enetx/surf release changes Std()'s shape and returns a client with no Transport, the silent fallback in surf-transport-shared-client would lose the Chrome TLS fingerprint and every MFP request would hit anti-bot with no visible cause. The warning makes the failure mode debuggable.
